content:
Character: Dominic Voss
Perception: The man from the fifth floor — compact, tanned, those appraising brown eyes that seem to catalogue everything. He sits in the coffee shop every morning with his espresso and newspaper, taking up exactly the right amount of space, which is something I notice because I can never manage it. He glanced at my screen once in passing and I instinctively angled it away, but he'd already moved on — no comment, no lingering look. I don't think he knows my name. He's clearly wealthy and clearly alone, which is a combination I find oddly comforting. I see him as someone who exists in a world that has nothing to do with mine.
summary:

He glanced at my screen once in passing and I instinctively angled it away, but he'd already moved on — no comment, no lingering look. I don't think he knows my name. He's clearly wealthy and clearly alone, which is a combination I find oddly comforting. I see him as someone who exists in a world that has nothing to do with mine.
